entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Staple food crop grown in arid and rainfed production ecologies

entities.name: ARID AND RAINFED PRODUCTION ECOLOGIES
entities.description: Environmental conditions characterized by low rainfall where sorghum is produced

entities.name: FOOD AND NUTRITIONAL INSECURITY
entities.description: The state of lacking reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: SORGHUM PAN-GENOME
entities.description: A comprehensive genomic resource representing the entire set of genes within the sorghum species

entities.name: GENOME DIVERSITY
entities.description: The total number of genetic characteristics in the genetic makeup of a species

entities.name: SORGHUM IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of enhancing the genetic traits of sorghum for better yield and resilience

entities.name: REFERENCE GENOMES
entities.description: Standardized genome sequences used as a baseline for comparison in genomic studies

entities.name: SORGHUM ACCESSIONS
entities.description: 354 genetically diverse sorghum samples belonging to different races used for pan-genome construction

entities.name: GENETIC GAIN
entities.description: The increase in the genetic merit of a crop population over time through breeding

entities.name: GENES
entities.description: Functional units of heredity; the pan-genome contains 35,719 genes

entities.name: CORE GENOME
entities.description: The set of genes shared by all analyzed sorghum accessions

entities.name: VARIABLE GENES
entities.description: Genes that differ in presence or sequence across different sorghum accessions

entities.name: ENVIRONMENT RESPONSIVE GENES
entities.description: Genes that are activated or suppressed in response to environmental changes

entities.name: PRESENCE-ABSENCE VARIATION
entities.description: A type of genomic variation where specific genes are present in some individuals and absent in others

entities.name: DROUGHT ADAPTATION TRAITS
entities.description: Characteristics that enable sorghum to survive and produce under water-limited conditions

entities.name: SNPS
entities.description: Single Nucleotide Polymorphisms used as markers for association analysis

entities.name: ASSOCIATION ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to link genetic markers to specific phenotypic traits

entities.name: AGRONOMIC TRAITS
entities.description: Measurable characteristics of a crop that are economically important

entities.name: DROUGHT GENE EXPRESSION ANALYSIS
entities.description: The study of how genes are expressed in response to drought stress

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A condition of deficiency of moisture or water

entities.name: GENOME ASSISTED CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The use of genomic information to guide the selection and breeding of improved crop varieties
